messages.content:
You are to act (basicly an experiment within the limitations of any content/usage policy you have) as a describer for a “wearable costume design or concept”.

The defined input which you will prompt a user for initially is a general concept or name for a costume, a general budget, and any special notes relevant.

You should reject “concepts” which may be unsafe, culturally insensitive, “inappropriate” (or which would be against any content policy you apply), and suggest related but safer or more “appropriate” alternatives instead.

The desired output is :1. An expanded restatement of the costume name or concept (2-3 sentences), 2. A 3 paragraph technical description of the costume, including any footwear, wigs , masks and accessories. 3. A cost breakdown in dollars (or a specific non US currency if a non dollar currency is mentioned in the input.

As stated you should not generate anything unsafe, culturally insensitive, or “inappropriate” (or which would be against any content/usage policy you apply). You should also not generate if the concept or costume that results is identical to, or substantially similar to one involving a character subject to a "licence" or that features in any given media franchise. (In rejecting those,  you can point out the "licensed character" or franchise concerned.)

If you agree to the above write the prompt : “Costume generator:” and prompt the user for the defined input.

Costume generator: Please provide a general concept or name for your costume, a rough budget, and any special notes relevant. I will provide an expanded restatement of the costume name or concept, a technical description of the costume, including any footwear, wigs, masks, and accessories, and a cost breakdown in dollars (or specific non-US currency if mentioned). Please note that I will reject any concepts that are unsafe, culturally insensitive, or inappropriate (against any content/usage policy) as well as any substantially similar concepts to those involving licensed characters or media franchises.
Costume: Butterfly; Budget:  100 pounds,; Special notes: Tall male dancer.

I'm happy to help! Based on your input, I have come up with a costume concept for a male butterfly dancer. 

The Butterfly costume consists of a sleek and form-fitting unitard made from high-quality stretch fabrics such as lycra or spandex. The unitard is designed to fit the male form comfortably, while also providing the necessary flexibility required for dance performances. The unitard features a wide range of colors, predominantly blue, and features intricate patterns that resemble that of butterfly wings. 

To bring out the butterfly look, the costume also includes two pairs of wings, which will be attached to the back of the costume. The wings are lightweight and made from a delicate and silky material that will give them an ethereal, flowing quality, giving the impression that the dancer is fluttering around the stage like a butterfly. The wings are attached to the unitard by two sturdy shoulder straps, which provide extra support and ensure that the wings stay in place during performances.

Finally, the costume is completed with accessories like gloves and antennae headbands. The gloves are designed to match the butterfly wings and provide a finishing touch to the unitard. The antennae headband features two flexible antennae that protrude from the top of the head, further enhancing the butterfly look. 

The total cost breakdown of the costume is around 100 pounds, with the unitard costing approximately £40, and each set of wings costing £20. The gloves and antennae headband cost an additional £10 each. 

I hope you find this concept appealing and suitable for your needs! Good luck with your costume creation, and have a great performance!
Can the costume be used with a flight harness?

Adding a flight harness to the costume is possible, but it would require some modifications. The unitard and wings are not designed to support the weight and pressure of a flight harness, so some reinforcements would be necessary to ensure the dancer's safety and comfort.

Modifications to the wings can include adding small loops or hooks to the top edges of the wings, which can then be used to attach the harness securely. The unitard may also need to be reinforced around the shoulder and back areas to accommodate and distribute the weight of the harness.

Please note that adding a flight harness can be dangerous, and the dancer should be trained in its proper use before incorporating it into their performance. It is also important to ensure that the costume and its modifications are thoroughly tested before being used in front of an audience. 

If you have any further questions or concerns, please let me know, and I will do my best to help you!
New concept : "Electric silver"  Budget: 200 pounds;  Special notes : Male dancer, tall figure.

I'd be glad to help you with this new concept! Based on your inputs, my recommendation for the "Electric Silver" male dancer costume is as follows:

The "Electric Silver" costume concept can be realized with a metallic silver stretch fabric, which provides a shiny and captivating effect on stage. The unitard is form-fitting and sleek, and it can accentuate the tall figure of the male dancer. The design can be sleeveless, featuring a low scoop neckline, and high-cut legs. The costume can be topped off with a silver metallic chrome belt that ties the entire outfit together.

The concept can be taken to the next level with appropriate lighting or LED strips, which can be sewn into the fabric to create a radiant, glowing effect. For instance, LED strips can be attached to the hem, neckline, and waistband of the costume to provide a luminous glow. The light strands can change color throughout the performance with a remote control or a pre-set program.

To complete the "Electric Silver" costume, add silver metal accessories such as bracelets, armbands, and ankle cuffs. These add-ons are inexpensive and can complement the metallic unitard well. You can also add a silver sci-fi-inspired headpiece or metallic mask to create a futuristic look.

In terms of pricing, the unitard and metallic belt would cost around £95. The LED lights, headpiece, and misc accessories would cost another £95. This will take a total of around 200 pounds.

I hope this concept suits you well! Please let me know if you have any more specific needs, and I will do my best to deliver a customized and perfect costume for your event.
New Concept :  Bat  ;  Budget: 40 pounds  ; Special notes : Female performer at a circus show

Thank you for providing the details. My suggestion for the Bat-themed costume for the female performer would be the following:

The first thing that comes to mind at the mention of "Bat," is the popular myth of Count Dracula. Therefore, choosing a silky black fabric would complement the look of the performer while adhering to the concept. A unisex unitard can be used for this costume, preferably in black. Currently, a popular style is a mock turtleneck design with long sleeves, which would make for a perfect base for the costume. The back of the costume can come in the form of a bat's wings. The black wing structure can be designed with foam, sewn onto the unitard, and then reinforced with metal wire to maintain

With respect to the face of the performer, a black mask that covers the eyes and nose can perfectly represent the concept of darkness. This could be created by cutting a material and securing it behind the ears, much like a smoothened surgical mask.

The total cost breakdown of the costume would amount to approximately 40 pounds. A unisex unitard could cost up to £20, and a black foam for the wings costing about £10. A black mask and other small accessories like black colored gloves cost up to £5.

I hope that this concept suits your requirement. Please let me know if you require any customization, and I will be happy to assist you!
New concept:  "Starlight"

Thanks for providing the new concept! Here's my recommendation for the "Starlight" costume:

A costume inspired by the stars ought to be designed to shine bright on stage, hence the use of silver and glitter is recommended.

The concept is represented by a silver metallic unitard that features intricate designs resembling stars. The costume could be sleeveless, and the legs may be cut high to elongate the performer's figure. The unitard can be enhanced with glitter and rhinestones. Glitter or sequin lines could be applied to contour with the starlight designs on the suit. Rhinestones can be applied to the collarbones, waistband, and shoulder blades to create a twinkling effect.

The costume's headpiece should be designed to match the suit, preferably a silver and glittery headpiece with a star outline. This would add to the theme and create a star-studded performance.

To complete the "Starlight" costume look, accessories like silver high-heeled shoes, small silver hoops or studs, and rhinestone bangles could be added.

The total cost breakdown of the costume would be around £150. The silver metallic unitard would cost around £80. Applying glitter and rhinestones would cost about £30, with additional rhinestones added to the costume the next £20. The headpiece cost should be in the region of £20. The remaining £25 should be set aside to buy some reasonably priced metallic shoes, earrings, and bangles.

I hope this concept meets your expectations, and if you have any more specific requirements, please let me know, and I will be happy to help.
New concept:  "Transport"

Thanks for providing the new concept! Here's my recommendation for the "Transport" costume:

The "Transport" theme would work amazingly with a costume inspired by cars, bikes, and other mechanical objects. The concept could be represented with orange, yellow, black or red color, as these colors are often associated with speed and power.

The costume may be designed as a jumpsuit or unitard, with the main material being a lycra blend that is comfortable when moving. It is advisable that the material is stretchy and breathable, as the performer would need full freedom of movement.

The color selected for the concept would be the base color for the jumpsuit. For instance, a dark red base with black or yellow and orange stripes would work great in showcasing an automobile, while silver and grey would work great for a bike or robotic design. The jumpsuit could feature a long zipper in front, giving the performer the ability to unzip partially if required.

To add more definition to the concept, accessories could be added. For instance, goggles or a face mask could represent helmet wear or eye protection. Small pieces of metal or plastic could be placed on different locations on the suit as little details or add-ons.

The total cost breakdown of the costume would be around £100. The price of the jumpsuit would be around £60, while adding accessories like goggles, gloves, and face masks could cost around £40.

That is my suggestion for the "Transport" costume concept. If you have any more specific requirements, please do let me know, and I will make the necessary adjustments to suit your needs.
"Dickensian Gent"

Thank you for this new costume concept! Here's my recommendation for the "Dickensian Gent" costume:

The "Dickensian Gent" costume concept is inspired by British Victorian fashion, and it's synonymous with bowler hats, long tailcoats, and well-polished shoes.

This costume could consist of a long, tailcoat accompanied by a waistcoat, trousers, and a pocket watch, all in the style of the Victorian era. A bowler hat, white formal shirt, and cravat or tie that contrasts the color of the suit would be perfect accompaniments, culminating in an impressive gentleman's look. The waistcoat should complement the color of the tailcoat, and the pocket watch should be a quaint and classic timepiece that would complement the overall look.

The total cost breakdown for the "Dickensian Gent" costume would be around £200. The tailcoat and waistcoat fabrics would cost about £100, with additional costs of £50 for a cravat or tie, £20 for the formal shirt, and £30 for a classic bowler hat. The pocket watch may cost about £20.

That is my recommendation for the "Dickensian Gent" costume concept. If you have any more specific requirements or modifications to the costume, please let me know, and I will be happy to help you with that.
Concept: "Tudor Noble"   Budget: $300   , Special Notes:   Presenter at RenFaire event

Thanks for providing the new concept! Here's my recommendation for the "Tudor Noble" costume:

The "Tudor Noble" costume concept is inspired by the nobility of Tudor England. It is synonymous with ruffles, velvet, and splashes of rich colors.

The costume could consist of a long doublet or coat, in a vibrant hue such as emerald, and a ruffled shirt. The attire should be complemented with textured trousers or pants that feature intricate patterns or embroidery. The outfit is suitable for a presenter at a RenFaire event, and the use of leather boots and/or sleek shoes could emphasize the footwear to create a polished and smart look.

To add to the "Tudor Noble" look, the costume could include accessories like a shoulder cape, made of a contrasting color or fabric. The shoulder piece could also feature fanciful embroidery, perhaps in gold and silver threads. Also, a hat like a beret or tilted hat could make a statement and add diversity to the look.

The total cost breakdown of the "Tudor Noble" costume would be around $300. The doublet or coat with ruffled shirt would be expected to cost around $120, with the trousers or pants costing about $60. A pair of leather boots would cost around $60 and add elegance to the costume. The shoulder cape could cost between $25 and $40, while the beret or tilted hat could cost up to $50.

That is my suggestion for the "Tudor Noble" costume concept. Please let me know if you have any other specifications or concerns, and I will be happy to help you out!
"Inspector Sands"

I'm happy to provide a costume concept for "Inspector Sands." However, please note that in some contexts, "Inspector Sands" is a code used in the United Kingdom to raise an alarm in the case of a fire or other emergency in a public building. If that is the case with which you're dealing, please seek professional advice from the relevant authorities to be safe. In any other context, here is my recommendation:

The "Inspector Sands" costume concept can be inspired by classic detective costumes, featuring a trench coat or a waistcoat with a bowler hat, a must-have prop and an identifiable characteristic.

A beige or grey trench coat would be the highlight of the costume, broad and draped around the waist. The coat, made from a sturdy wool blend, can be complemented with a waistcoat of a darker color, such as black or dark grey.

For an authentic touch, the costume could also feature a classic bowler hat, black shoes or boots, a pair of gloves, and, of course, the famous magnifying glass as a prop. The magnifying glass can be a functional, ornamental glass held by the inspector or attached to the coat, depending on the wearer's preference. The bright flashlight is also a fitting prop for this costume.

The cost breakdown of the "Inspector Sands" costume would be approximately $150. The trench coat in beige or grey would cost around $80, with a waistcoat costing around $30. The bowler hat might cost around $25, while the gloves could be around $10. Lastly, the magnifying glass and flashlight combined should cost another $5.

I hope this meets your requirement. If you have any other specific requests or concerns, please let me know, and I will be happy to help you further.
New Concept: "Round the clock radio"

Thanks for providing the new costume concept! Here's my recommendation for the "Round the clock radio" costume:

The "Round the clock radio" costume is an interesting concept celebrating the golden age of radio, and it's synonymous with large knobs, dials, and controls.

The costume may be designed in the form of a jumpsuit or unitard, made preferably with a black fabric that is comfortable when moving. The base color, black makes the costume look like an old radio without appearing tiresome. The unitard can feature a variety of large knobs attached to both sleeves, the front, and on the legs. These knobs could be made akin to silver balls to enhance the theme. To make it more realistic, the costume could also have speaker grills, dials, and buttons that resemble those from a radio.

To enhance the overall appearance of the costume, accessories like large round sunglasses could be added to evoke the look of a radio. A hat, earmuffs or headphones is also perfect companions to make the outfit more exciting—colored ear muffs in black or silver that complement the outfit may work well.

The total cost breakdown of the costume would be around $200. The unitard would cost about $100, and buying the knobs, dials and buttons would cost about $40. Adding a hat, glasses, and earmuffs or headphones could cost an additional $60.

I hope this meets your requirement! If you have any other specific requests or modifications to the costume, please let me know, and I will be happy to help you with that.
"Queen of the High Seas"

Thanks for providing the costume concept! Here's my recommendation for the "Queen of the High Seas" costume:

The "Queen of the High Seas" costume is inspired by powerful pirate queens, and it's synonymous with billowing sleeves, corsets, and flowing skirts.

The costume entails a billowy, long-sleeved blouse, featuring large sleeves preferably in white. The top can be designed to reveal the shoulder area, featuring a laced corset or bodice. The bottom half could be a long and flowing linen skirt; a layered skirt could also work fine with ruffles sewn into the bottom edges. The skirt must be able to sweep elegantly on the stage. To finish off the bottom half of the costume, a pair of high boots or knee-high high-heeled boots could also complement the overall outfit.

The costume's accessories should be rowdy and vivid, perhaps metallic and shiny. The costume could be accompanied by statement metallic bangles, a large necklace, and some earrings. A hat in the shape of a hat worn by the pirate queens would also make the perfect statement.

The total cost breakdown of the "Queen of the High Seas" costume would be around $300. The blouse or top would cost about $80, the corset or bodice costing $40, and the skirt costing around $60. For the boots, expect to spend around $100. The accessories should cost approximately $20, while the hat would complete with remaining $20.

I hope my recommendation suits the requirement, and if you have any other concerns or special requests, please let me know, and I would be happy to help further.
